description: Implement the advanced threat intel components: MITRE ATT&CK Integration, CVE Database, Threat Correlation Engine, IOC Matching Engine, and Threat Scoring system.
steps: Create src/threat_intel/include/threat_intel/MitreAttack.h and src/threat_intel/src/MitreAttack.cpp - Define Tactic struct (id, name, description, techniques list), Technique struct (id, name, description, tactics, dataSources, detection). MitreAttackMatrix class with: addTechnique, getTechniqueById (e.g., T1059), getTechniquesByTactic, getAllTactics, mapDetectionToTechnique(detectionInfo) -> vector<Technique>, searchTechniques(query). Pre-populate with common ATT&CK techniques in constructor (at least 20 entries covering Initial Access, Execution, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion, etc.)

Create src/threat_intel/include/threat_intel/CVEDatabase.h and src/threat_intel/src/CVEDatabase.cpp - Define CVEEntry struct (cveId, description, cvssScore, severity, affectedProducts vector with vendor/product/version, publishDate, lastModified, references). CVEDatabase class with: addCVE, getCVEById, searchByProduct, searchByVendor, filterBySeverity(minScore), getRecentCVEs(days), updateCVE. Thread-safe.

Create src/threat_intel/include/threat_intel/ThreatCorrelation.h and src/threat_intel/src/ThreatCorrelation.cpp - Define Campaign struct (id, name, iocIds vector, techniques vector, threatActors, confidence, firstSeen, lastSeen, description). CorrelationRule struct (name, iocTypes to correlate, timeWindow, minIOCs). ThreatCorrelationEngine class with: addCorrelationRule, correlateIOCs(iocs) -> vector<Campaign>, findRelatedIOCs(iocId) -> vector of related IOCs with relationship type, calculateCorrelationScore(campaign) -> double

Create src/threat_intel/include/threat_intel/IOCMatcher.h and src/threat_intel/src/IOCMatcher.cpp - IOCMatcher class with: loadIOCs(from IOCManager), matchExact(value) for hash/email exact matching using unordered_map, matchDomain(domain) with suffix matching for domain IOCs, matchIP(ip) with prefix trie for IP/CIDR matching, matchURL(url), matchAll(indicator) -> vector<MatchResult> with matched IOC, match type, confidence. Use efficient data structures (hash maps for exact, simple trie node struct for prefix).

Create src/threat_intel/include/threat_intel/ThreatScoring.h and src/threat_intel/src/ThreatScoring.cpp - Define ThreatScore struct (overallScore 0-100, iocConfidence, sourceReliability, ageDecay, correlationBoost, breakdown map). ThreatScoringEngine class with: calculateScore(ioc) -> ThreatScore considering confidence, source reliability weights, age decay formula (newer = higher), correlation boost if IOC is part of campaign. configureWeights(map of factor->weight). Scoring formula: base = confidence * 100, apply source weight multiplier, apply age decay (exponential decay over 90 days), add correlation boost if correlated.
Update src/threat_intel/CMakeLists.txt to include all new .cpp files
acceptance_criteria: MitreAttackMatrix can look up techniques by ID (T1059, T1053, etc.) and map tactics to techniques
CVEDatabase supports CRUD and search by product/vendor/severity with CVSS scoring
ThreatCorrelationEngine identifies campaigns from related IOCs and scores correlation strength
IOCMatcher efficiently matches indicators using hash-based exact match and trie-based prefix matching
ThreatScoringEngine produces scores 0-100 factoring in confidence, source, age, and correlation
verification: Confirm no compilation errors for the updated threat_intel module
findings: All 5 components compiled cleanly with no warnings. Tests pass. The MitreAttackMatrix has 25 techniques across 11 tactics pre-populated. The IOCMatcher uses a trie with character-level traversal for IP prefix matching. Age decay uses exponential formula with 45-day half-life. The goto statement in ThreatCorrelation.cpp findRelatedIOCs is used for nested loop break pattern.
